_meta.description: Curated snapshot of public spaceflight materials data. Outgassing values are representative entries in the style of the NASA GSFC outgassing database (outgassing.nasa.gov); TML/CVCM depend on lot, cure, and bakeout, so always verify against the database entry for the exact product/cure before flight use. Cryogenic curves are representative point data from public literature (NIST cryogenic material properties program, NASA technical reports); they are NOT design allowables. Room-temperature strengths are typical handbook values, not MMPDS A/B-basis allowables.
sources: NASA GSFC Outgassing Data for Selecting Spacecraft Materials, https://outgassing.nasa.gov
ASTM E595 Standard Test Method for Total Mass Loss and Collected Volatile Condensable Materials
NASA-STD-6001 Flammability, Offgassing, and Compatibility Requirements

materials.name: Stainless Steel 304L

outgassing.source: metal; non-outgassing per E595 screening practice

yield_strength_mpa.source: representative annealed 304L cryogenic tensile data, NIST/NASA literature

ultimate_strength_mpa.source: austenitic strain-hardening at cryogenic temperature; representative

materials.notes: Workhorse cryo-tank alloy; retains toughness to 4 K. Annealed values; cold work raises strength substantially.

materials.name: Stainless Steel 301

outgassing.source: metal; non-outgassing per E595 screening practice

materials.notes: Cold-rolled tempers give very high cryogenic strength-to-weight for stainless; martensitic transformation under strain increases strength at cryo.

materials.name: Al-Li 2195

outgassing.source: metal; non-outgassing per E595 screening practice

yield_strength_mpa.source: representative 2195-T8 cryogenic tensile data (Space Shuttle ET / SLS literature)

materials.notes: Strength increases monotonically down to 20 K; used on Super Lightweight Tank. Weld allowables are substantially lower than parent metal.

materials.name: Inconel 718

outgassing.source: metal; non-outgassing per E595 screening practice

materials.notes: Standard for cryogenic fasteners, bellows, and engine hardware; ductile to 4 K.

materials.name: PTFE (Teflon)

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Excellent outgassing and chemical compatibility; large thermal contraction and cold flow under load.

materials.name: PEEK

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: High-performance structural thermoplastic; usable at cryogenic temperature though it embrittles.

materials.name: Kapton H film

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; moisture dominates TML

materials.notes: MLI and flex-circuit staple; TML is mostly recoverable water (high WVR).

materials.name: Mylar A film

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: MLI layer film; flammable in oxygen-enriched environments, embrittles below ~200 K less than most polyesters.

materials.name: Vespel SP-1

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; fails TML unless baked out

materials.notes: Marginal on TML (water-dominated); commonly accepted after vacuum bakeout with WVR credit.

materials.name: Ultem 1000

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Good outgassing and inherent flame resistance; common for electrical isolators.

materials.name: Nylon 6/6

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; fails TML

materials.notes: Fails E595 TML due to absorbed water; generally avoided in vacuum hardware.

materials.name: Delrin (acetal)

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Passes outgassing but burns readily; flammability usually disqualifies it for crewed-vehicle use.

materials.name: G-10/FR-4 glass epoxy

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; varies by resin lot

thermal_conductivity_w_mk.source: NIST cryogenic material properties, G-10 normal direction

materials.notes: Standard cryogenic structural insulator/standoff; well-characterized to 4 K.

materials.name: RTV 566

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result, low-volatility space-grade silicone

materials.notes: Low-outgassing space-grade RTV; silicone contamination risk near optics regardless of CVCM.

materials.name: RTV 560

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; CVCM near the 0.10 limit

materials.notes: Usable but CVCM is borderline; prefer 566 near contamination-sensitive surfaces.

materials.name: Scotch-Weld EC-2216 B/A

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Classic cryo-tolerant structural adhesive; retains toughness at 77 K better than rigid epoxies.

materials.name: Hysol EA 9394

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: High-strength paste adhesive used in launch vehicle bonding.

materials.name: Stycast 2850FT

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Cryogenic potting standard; thermal contraction matched to metals better than unfilled epoxies.

materials.name: Braycote 601EF

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result

materials.notes: Default vacuum grease for spaceflight mechanisms.

materials.name: Apiezon N

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; fails both limits

materials.notes: Excellent cryogenic thermal-contact grease for lab use, but fails E595 badly — keep out of flight vacuum hardware.

materials.name: Aeroglaze Z306

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; TML at the limit, bakeout-sensitive

materials.notes: Standard black thermal-control/stray-light coating; requires bakeout, TML right at the 1.0 limit.

materials.name: Viton A

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; compound-dependent

materials.notes: Common seal material; glass transition ~ 250 K, NOT usable as a cryogenic seal.

materials.name: Buna-N (nitrile)

outgassing.source: GSFC-style representative E595 result; fails both limits

materials.notes: Fails E595 and flammability; ground-support use only.
